response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's two most critical requirements. His C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; his one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was a German fintech investment platform, not investment banking or capital markets infrastructure. His core expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, none of which appear in the job requirements.
strengths: Long career with strong communication skills demonstrated through public talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
CI/CD and DevOps experience (Docker, GitLab, Kubernetes) aligns with optional desirable skills
Exposure to high-performance, low-latency systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) shows systems-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, circa 2007–2008) — does not meet the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No Windows platform development history; work is predominantly Linux and web-based
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge
